content:
Actually, I don't have access to the internet. My responses are generated based on a large amount of text data that I was trained on, which includes books, articles, websites, and other written sources up until my knowledge cutoff date (June 2024). I use patterns in that data to generate answers to your questions.

When people say an AI "has access to the internet," they usually mean the AI can look up real-time information online. However, I don't have that capability. Instead, I rely on the information I was trained on and the knowledge embedded in my model. This means that while I can provide detailed and accurate answers on a wide range of topics, I can't browse the web or access new information beyond my training data.

If you ever hear otherwise, it might be referring to a different system or a version of an AI that is connected to live internet searches, but that is not how I operate.
